2026-04-13T10:43:49.2132530Z 
2026-04-13T10:43:50.5063923Z **CI failures found on HEAD commit (`1cff9ac9baf9c972d75b3fba2be36b694554d55e`):**
2026-04-13T10:43:50.5064470Z 
2026-04-13T10:43:51.5379716Z 1. **`js - openai (Build Build)`** — Fails with `Path does not exist: /mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish` and `Manifest generation failed`. This is an artifact-packaging pipeline step, NOT a unit test. The openai `ci.yml` explicitly sets `RunUnitTests: false`, so no unit tests run in CI for this package. The `Build Analyze` job (linting) succeeded, meaning the TypeScript source compiles.
2026-04-13T10:43:51.5381016Z 
2026-04-13T10:43:52.6662277Z 2. **`js - resources - mgmt (Build Build)`** — Same error pattern. But all four `Build UnitTest` jobs passed (49/49 tests).
2026-04-13T10:43:52.6662971Z 
2026-04-13T10:43:52.6673659Z 3. **`js - attestation`** — All 151 tests passed (0 failed). Failure is in `Integration PublishDocsToNightlyBranch` (doc publishing step), not a test job.
2026-04-13T10:43:52.6674320Z 
2026-04-13T10:43:53.8888610Z All `- tests`, `- tests-weekly`, and `- perf` runs are excluded per the task rules.
2026-04-13T10:43:53.8889106Z 
2026-04-13T10:43:57.8733433Z **Conclusion:** There are no unit test failures on `main`. The CI failures are in build artifact packaging steps and doc publishing integration steps — none in `(Build UnitTest ...)` jobs. All unit tests that ran passed.
2026-04-13T10:43:57.8734625Z

2026-04-13T10:44:05.8547081Z

2026-04-13T10:44:05.8547914Z   
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8549493Z   The build itself probably failed before it could create those publish packages. Looking at the "Build Build" job across both projects - for `js - resources - mgmt`, the UnitTests passed but the Build Build failed, and for openai, there's no UnitTest job but Build Build also failed. In Azure SDK pipelines, the "Build" job typically handles packaging and linting, so this error about the missing publish directory suggests the build step didn't complete successfully before trying to package for publishing.
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8551292Z   
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8552329Z   The same "Path does not exist" and "Manifest generation failed" errors are showing up across multiple packages - both `js - openai` and `js - resources - mgmt` - which points to a shared infrastructure problem rather than individual package issues.
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8553130Z

2026-04-13T10:44:05.8556374Z

2026-04-13T10:44:05.8557276Z   2. `js - resources - mgmt (Build Build)` - Pipeline packaging failure (unit tests all passed)
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8557830Z   3. `js - attestation (Integration PublishDocsToNightlyBranch)` - Doc publishing failure

2026-04-13T10:44:05.8558944Z

2026-04-13T10:44:05.8560760Z   
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8562177Z   Looking at the commit history, none of the recent changes seem directly related to resources or openai packages, so I need to check the actual timing of when these builds were triggered. 2026-04-13T10:44:05.8563563Z   
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8566878Z   For resources, all 49 tests passed. For attestation, all 151 tests passed. The openai package didn't even run unit tests since they're disabled. The errors are in the build and packaging steps themselves, not in test execution. So technically these aren't unit test failures at all, which makes me question whether I should even be creating an issue for this. But the openai build failure is still a real CI problem worth investigating, so let me check if there's a local build issue with that package. I'm realizing these failures in both packages on the same day point to an infrastructure issue rather than actual code problems. The missing "packages-dev-publish" path suggests a transient pipeline glitch, especially since the openai build appears to have completed successfully without compilation errors—the failure only happened in the downstream packaging step.
2026-04-13T10:44:05.8569522Z

The automated [Analyze CI Test Failures](.github/workflows/fix-test-failures.md) workflow uses this issue to skip known failures and avoid filing duplicate issues.\\n\\n## Known Failures\\n\\n_No known CI (playback) failures at this time._\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This list only tracks failures from **CI pipelines** (`ci.yml`, playback mode). Live-test pipeline failures (`tests.yml`) are excluded — the workflow filters them out by check-run name.\\n\\n## Maintenance Guidelines\\n\\n- **Adding entries:** When a CI failure is confirmed to be a pre-existing infrastructure/service issue (not a code bug), add it here with the package name, test pattern, error summary, root cause, and a tracking issue link.\\n- **Removing entries:** When the underlying issue is fixed and CI is green again, remove the entry and close the corresponding tracking issue.\\n- **Format:** Each entry must include the **package name** (as it appears in check-run names) so the workflow can match it.\\n- **Review cadence:** Review this list monthly to check if any known failures have been resolved.\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/37864\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"deyaaeldeen\",\"id\":6074665,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/deyaaeldeen\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6074665?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"test-reliability\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-27T04:32:04Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-27T16:33:26Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
